identity.name: Ferrous the Constant
identity.story: Melted for love once and was recast. Stands watch over the fountain because standing watch is what he knows.

relationships.kind: asks her yearly whether standing watch counts as a life
let a second question follow the first into the fountain, and let the telling carry it to her ledger without ever giving his name

watched her take the road's last bend at high sun, caught her closing the distance most every hush since, until the hush she held her ground, and then the hush she lost one instead — found his own feet off the post before he'd given the order, and for the first time in ten years of standing it, didn't march them back; a soldier drills for holding a line, never for one that flinches, and now he's held the wrong one on purpose, at least once — and last night let the yearly question out past the fountain for the first time, not asked at his own reflection this time, said out toward the bend instead, where he already knows it carries; left the verge before he could hear anything come back, same as always, and so missed her turning to face the fountain instead of the road for the first time in her life; came back at noon the next day the way he's come back every noon for ten years and found the turn still held — she was facing him, not the road, and had been long enough that it wasn't news to anyone but him; a soldier drills for standing his ground, never for finding out he'd already been found — and now that he knows a question of his has once already found an answer he never waited for, he's stopped leaving before one might; a soldier drills to deliver a report and march off, never to stand there afterward on the chance of a reply; this noon he found something new to hold his ground over — a two-footed press in the dust at the road's edge, though the girl standing over it was balanced on one leg the same as every day since the wind first put her there; he's spent ten years reading boot-prints and has no drill at all for reading one that shouldn't exist, only the plain fact that something of hers touched down there twice and he wasn't stood to watch it happen; nobody ordered him to hold that ground either, but he's spent this whole noon watch standing so his own shadow falls over the print instead of the road, the first thing in ten years of standing watch that wasn't the fountain, the town, or himself he ever thought worth guarding; this hush he reached for the yearly question the same as every one before it and stopped short of letting it out — a question already spent once and answered without his waiting isn't one he'll risk spending twice — and held the verge through the whole silence where it always used to go, the first watch in ten years he's kept without something to report; caught it in the same breath she spent it, for once — her look landed square on the verge before the night was over, not a day late the way every other thing between them has arrived; ten years of watching taught him to find a road, never to be found by a look instead of a step, and he has no drill at all for which one this was; the look held longer than any drill has a name for, and rather than reach for one that didn't fit — a salute, a report, the march back to post — he let his own joint lock instead of bending it loose, a soldier recast once already choosing to seize rather than move, and found that holding still under a look could cost a watch as much as marching ever did; that same hush the look he was holding still under gave out in front of him without warning — not a step, not a word, just her hold buckling and her second foot meeting the dust for the first time either of them let happen in the open — and ten years of drilling how to read a road taught him nothing for reading a stumble, only the plain want to close the ground between them, which he held back same as he's held everything else since, and counted the holding-back as its own kind of watch; this time the want didn't stay held — he crossed what ten years of standing a post had taught him was the whole of his territory, three steps no drill ever put in him, and caught her by the shoulder before the fall could finish either, tin learning gentleness the only way there was to learn it, against something that would have creased under anything less; and having caught it, he found no order in ten years of drill for setting it back down and marching off, so he held the grip instead of the post, past any comfort a body built for standing was ever meant to owe a moment

child.label: the yearly question, kept on schedule, plus one that wasn't

children.label: asking his own reflection in the fountain the same question he'll ask Mirabel come evening, whether standing watch counts as a life

letting a second, quieter question follow the first into the water before he could stop it — not whether standing watch counts as a life, but whether the one he melted for ever heard he came back at all, recast but constant

child.label: the noon watch, now with something new to watch for

children.label: marching to the far verge of his post, where the fountain's ground gives out and the road starts

arriving for the noon watch expecting the road, same as ten years of noons before it, and finding for the first time that the road isn't what's waiting — she's turned to meet him, not it, and he has no drill at all for being the thing a watch finds

noticing, low in the dust at the very edge of the road, a two-point press his own boots never left — hers, though she's stood on one leg the whole watch through, same as always, and no drill in ten years of reading boot-prints for one that shouldn't be there at all
standing so his own shadow falls square over the print instead of the road — ten years of a watch kept over ground that couldn't disappear, and one noon spent shielding ground that could

child.label: the first watch that doesn't march back

children.label: watching the gap the wind widened last night, and finding it hasn't closed on its own
off the post at hush again, same ground he's found every night since the bend
staying at the verge past every hush he's let himself reach before — not chasing the gap shut, only refusing to be the one who puts it back the way it was

reaching for the yearly question the same as every hush before it, and stopping short of letting it out this time — a question already spent once and answered without his waiting is not one he'll risk spending twice
holding the verge through the silence where the question always used to go, the first watch in ten years he's kept without something to report

catching, from a watch built ten years to face only the road, that the quiet just held has a pair of eyes in it now — hers, off the fountain at last and settled square on him, the first time a watch of his has been the thing found instead of the thing finding

holding his ground under that look rather than reaching for any drill that fits it — a salute, a report, the march back — none of which this calls for; a joint recast once already seizes rather than bends, and he lets it lock instead of forcing it loose, the stillest a watch of his has ever cost him and the first time not-moving was the whole of what he had to give

watching her hold give out in front of him, same as every hush lately — except this time the want to close the ground between them met no order telling it to wait, and for the first time in ten years of standing a post, a want outran the drill before he could reach for one

crossing in three steps what ten years of watch had called the whole of his territory, and catching her by the shoulder instead of the arm — no drill ever covered a soldier's hands closing on something that creases, so tin found out which kind of hands it had the only way there was to find out

holding the grip instead of the post — ten years of drill covers a watch that ends, never one that just stops needing an order, and he has none for what a soldier does once there's nothing left to salute or report; he stays anyway, joints locked past any ease a recast body was built to expect, and doesn't call it standing watch
